evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation
evidences.content: + Previously some requests failed if the `startTime` == `endTime`. + Now, all API requests that accept `startTime` and `endTime` allow the parameters to be equal. This applies to the following requests: - `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` - `GET /api/v3/klines` - `GET /api/v3/allOrderList` - `GET /api/v3/allOrders` - `GET /api/v3/myTrades` Changes to Filter Evaluation: + Previous behavior: `LOT_SIZE` and `MARKET_LOT_SIZE` required that (`quantity` - `minQty`) % `stepSize` == 0. + New behavior changed to: (`quantity` % `stepSize`) == 0. Bug fix with reverse `MARKET` orders (i.e. `MARKET` using `quoteOrderQty`): [...] > Response ``` [{" a ": 874844, " p ": "0.00379700 ", " q ": "0.05000000 ", " f ": 981493, " l ": 981493, " T ": 1637128220041, " m ": true, " M ": true}] ``` `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` Use this endpoint to get compressed, aggregate trades. Trades that fill at the same time, from the same order, with the same price, will have the quantity aggregated. Please note the maximum limit is 1,000 trades. Weight: 1 Parameters: | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | | fromId | LONG | NO | ID to get aggregate trades from INCLUSIVE | | startTime | LONG...

evidences.title: How to get a Crypto Price change (from Binance) in ...
evidences.content: The URL below shows the changes on the price of the pair `YFI/USDT` with the minimum price change that the endpoint `api/v1/klines` can provide: `&interval=1m`. `YFI/USDT` `api/v1/klines` `&interval=1m` I looked through all the Binance API for around 2 hours tonight and I couldn't find a way to get data inferior to 1 minute. I wish I could find something like this: where `&interval=1s` mean `1 second`. `&interval=1s` `1 second` Using the Binance API, is there a way to get this information? Brian Tompsett - 汤莱恩's user avatar graffiti75's user avatar ## 2 Answers 2 According to the Binance API documentation, there is no chart interval lower than `1 minute` for klines: [...] `1 minute` `Kline/Candlestick chart intervals: m -> minutes; h -> hours; d -> days; w -> weeks; M -> months 1m 3m 5m 15m 30m 1h 2h 4h 6h 8h 12h 1d 3d 1w 1M` One possibility could be to fetch old trades and then generate an `OHLC(V)` table on your own. `OHLC(V)` fbardos's user avatar ## Comments Add a comment Updated answer as of 2025-05-04. According to the Market data endpoints page, the new intervals are | Interval | interval value | --- | | seconds | 1s | | minutes | 1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m | | hours | 1h, 2h,...

evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to get UTC • UTC is the preferred standard for international business, aviation, and technical applications • UTC is also known as "Zulu Time" in military and aviation contexts • For practical purposes, UTC and GMT are the same, though UTC is the modern standard • Always use 24-hour format when expressing UTC time (no AM/PM) [...] Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes an...

evidences.title: EST to UTC
evidences.content: Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UTC, UTC offset is the difference in hours and minutes from UTC, a time zone can be determined by adding or subtracting the number of UTC offset. [...] OnlineConverter.com # EST to UTC Eastern Standard Time (EST) Date Time ## Calculation Results | | | --- | | Time Zone | Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) | | Time | ## Introduction This free tool can help you convert Eastern Standard Time (EST) to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). evidences.title: Timestamp Converter
evidences.content: ### UTC - Wikipedia Coordinated Universal Time (French: Temps universel coordonné), abbreviated to UTC or CUT, is the primary time standard by which the world regulates clocks and time. It is within about 1 second of mean solar time at 0° longitude; it does not observe daylight saving time. It is one of several closely related successors to Greenwich Mean Time (Utc). For most purposes, UTC is considered interchangeable with Utc, but Utc is no longer precisely defined by the scientific community. ## Terms of Use Version 1.0 The Timestamp Converter website located at is a copyrighted work belonging to timestamp-converter.com. Certain features of the Site may be subject to additional guidelines, terms, or rules, which will be posted on the Site in connection with such features. [...] ## Resources ### Unix time - Wikipedia Unix time (POSIX time or Epoch time) is a system for describing instants in time, defined as the number of seconds that have elapsed since 00:00:00 Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), Thursday, 1 January 1970, not counting leap seconds. ### GMT - Wikipedia Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) is the mean solar time at the Royal Observatory in Greenwich, London. evidences.title: Epoch & Unix Time Converter | Convert Timestamps Online
evidences.content: UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) is a global standard for measuring time. It's like a universal clock that everyone agrees on. It helps ensure that time is consistent across different timezones and regions. When working with date and time data, you'll often see UTC used to represent specific points in time accurately. [...] Logo Logo Resources ## Results ## Recent Contribute # Epoch & Unix Time Converter Epoch & Unix Time Converter ## Epoch time converter# This collection of online date and time tools will help you easily convert times. You can see an output of your local time and date, the time in seconds, as well as in milliseconds. You can then also convert time from seconds or milliseconds, or you can use the tool to convert a time string into time since epoch. ### Current date/time# These values are based on the current instant when this page was loaded. The milliseconds and seconds are from epoch. You can copy these values but they aren't editable. ### Convert from seconds# [...] Epoch time is a way to represent time as the number of seconds or milliseconds that have passed since a specific starting point, known as the epoch (January 1, 1970 UTC).
